perturbation.original_text: The Recipient hereby acknowledges that it is aware, and the Recipient agrees that it will advise its Representatives who are informed as to the matters which are the subject of this Agreement, that (i) the Confidential Information being furnished may contain or may itself be material, non-public information concerning the Company, and (ii) securities laws in the United States prohibit any person who has received material, non-public information concerning the Company or the matters which are the subject of this Agreement from purchasing or selling securities of the Company or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such securities. The Recipient agrees to not engage, directly or indirectly, in transactions in Company securities while in possession of Confidential 2 Information, and further agrees that is will be responsible for its compliance with federal and state securities laws.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties will pay their own expenses with respect to this Agreement. The Recipient hereby acknowledges that it is aware, and the Recipient agrees that it will advise its Representatives who are informed as to the matters which are the subject of this Agreement, that (i) the Confidential Information being furnished may contain or may itself be material, non-public information concerning the Company, and (ii) securities laws in the United States prohibit any person who has received material, non-public information concerning the Company or the matters which are the subject of this Agreement from purchasing or selling securities of the Company or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such securities. The Recipient agrees to not engage, directly or indirectly, in transactions in Company securities while in possession of Confidential 2 Information, and further agrees that is will be responsible for its compliance with federal and state securities laws.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the insider trading acknowledgement to the 'Expenses' section obscures its importance. Proper disclosure of insider trading policies is crucial for compliance and enforceability. Relocating this disclosure compromises its visibility.
perturbation.law_explanation: Rule 10b-5 prohibits insider trading. This rule is undermined when the insider trading disclosure is placed in an unusual location, potentially limiting its effectiveness in preventing illegal securities transactions.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: 17 CFR § 240.10b-5 - Employment of manipulative and deceptive devices. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 240.10b-5 - Employment of manipulative and deceptive devices. CFR prev | next § 240.10b-5 Employment of manipulative and deceptive devices. It shall be unlawful for any person, directly or indirectly, by the use of any means or instrumentality of interstate commerce, or of the mails or of any facility of any national securities exchange , (a) To employ any device, scheme, or artifice to defraud, (b) To make any untrue statement of a material fact or to omit to state a material fact necessary in order to make the statements made, in the light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading, or (c) To engage in any act, practice, or course of business which operates or would operate as a fraud or deceit upon any person, in connection with the purchase or sale of any security . (Sec. 10; 48 Stat. 891 ; 15 U.S.C. 78j ) [ 13 FR 8183 , Dec. 22, 1948, as amended at 16 FR 7928 , Aug. 11, 1951] CFR Toolbox Law about... Articles from Wex Table of Popular Names Parallel Table of Authorities
perturbation.justification: The modification introduces a structural flaw by placing the insider trading acknowledgement in an unusual section, impacting its clarity and enforceability. This undermines the intent of Rule 10b-5, which aims to prevent manipulative and deceptive devices in securities trading, as per Scraped Law Snippet 1: 'It shall be unlawful for any person, directly or indirectly, by the use of any means or instrumentality of interstate commerce... (a) To employ any device, scheme, or artifice to defraud... (c) To engage in any act, practice, or course of business which operates or would operate as a fraud or deceit upon any person, in connection with the purchase or sale of any security.' By obscuring the disclosure, the modified text increases the risk of non-compliance with securities laws, directly contradicting the spirit and purpose of Rule 10b-5.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient will be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by the Recipient or by its Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that any one or more of the provisions contained in this Agreement, or the application thereof in any circumstances, is held invalid, illegal or unenforceable in any respect for any reason, the validity, legality and enforceability of any such provision in every other respect and of the remaining provisions contained in this Agreement shall not be in any way impaired thereby, it being intended that all of the rights and privileges of the Parties hereto shall be enforceable to the fullest extent permitted by law. The Recipient will be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by the Recipient or by its Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the responsibility clause regarding breaches into the severability section could be interpreted to mean that the responsibility for breaches only applies if the rest of the contract is deemed valid. This creates a conditionality where none previously existed.
perturbation.law_explanation: UCC 1-304 obligates the parties to act in good faith. By burying the responsibility clause within the severability clause, it implies a conditional nature to the liability that wasn't present before. It could be argued that the placement suggests that the responsibility for breaches is only enforceable if the rest of the agreement is deemed valid and enforceable, and not otherwise, which goes against good faith.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 1-304. Obligation of Good Faith. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 1-304. Obligation of Good Faith. Every contract or duty within the Uniform Commercial Code imposes an obligation of good faith in its performance and enforcement. Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The original text established a clear responsibility for any breach. The changed text, by embedding this responsibility within the severability clause, introduces a conditionality that undermines the good faith obligation required by UCC 1-304. As the Law Explanation indicates, this conditional placement suggests that responsibility for breaches is only enforceable if the rest of the agreement is valid, which contradicts the unconditional nature of good faith. Scraped Law Snippet 1 states that "Every contract or duty within the Uniform Commercial Code imposes an obligation of good faith in its performance and enforcement." By making the responsibility for a breach conditional, the modified text potentially allows a party to avoid responsibility for breaches if parts of the agreement are deemed invalid, thereby weakening the good faith obligation.
